name: Castle of La Mota Loop
name_original: City tour por Medina del Campo
description: Explore Medina del Campo's historical sites on this easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) jogging route, passing landmarks like La Mota Castle.
region_intro: Jogging around Medina Del Campo offers a blend of historical landscapes and natural environments. The region features green tracks like the Camino de las Salinas, which winds through open fields and pine areas. While the Zapardiel River has an intermittent flow, the broader Castilian countryside provides varied terrain, including pine forests and lagoons. The historic La Mota Castle, situated on a hill, also offers established routes with diverse backdrops for running. Best jogging routes around Medina Del Campo The most popular jogging route is Castle of La Mota Loop, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes about 25 minutes to complete. This easy route circles the historic La Mota Castle, offering views of the town. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Balneario de Las Salinas, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This route traverses open fields and pine areas, characteristic of the Camino de las Salinas. Local runners also love the Palacio de Dueñas Courtyard loop from Medina del Campo, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through the town's historical architecture, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Jogging around Medina Del Campo is defined by green tracks, historic castle surroundings, and the peaceful Castilian countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to more challenging running routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 160 runners have used komoot to explore Medina Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Medina del Campo?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Medina del Campo documented on komoot, offering a variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Medina del Campo?
A: Yes, Medina del Campo offers several easy jogging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120211519/castle-of-la-mota-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) trail that circles the historic castle, providing scenic views without significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while running in Medina del Campo?
A: Jogging in Medina del Campo offers a blend of historical and natural scenery. You can expect to run through open fields, tranquil pine areas, and along green tracks like the Camino de las Salinas. The routes also often feature views of the Castilian countryside and historical architecture, including the majestic La Mota Castle.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging run, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of San Antolín – Royal Testamentary Palace loop from Medina del Campo AV&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, more demanding option, covering nearly 12.2 miles (19.7 km) with moderate elevation.

Q: Can I find circular jogging routes in Medina del Campo?
A: Many of the running routes in Medina del Campo are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120211519/castle-of-la-mota-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Balneario de Las Salinas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: Medina del Campo's routes often pass by significant historical sites. You can run around the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota&lt;/a&gt;, or through areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5621377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of San Antolín&lt;/a&gt;, offering a cultural dimension to your run.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly good for evening jogs?
A: The Camino de las Salinas, a popular green track, is equipped with streetlights along its route, making it suitable for evening runs. This allows for safe and enjoyable jogging even after sunset.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Medina del Campo?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the varied terrain and scenic backdrops of Medina del Campo. Runners particularly enjoy the well-maintained green tracks, the peaceful Castilian countryside, and the unique experience of running around historic landmarks like La Mota Castle.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of urban and natural environments?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Dueñas Courtyard loop from Medina del Campo&lt;/a&gt; lead through the town's historical architecture before transitioning into more natural surroundings, offering a diverse running experience.

Q: Can I find routes that are suitable for longer distance running?
A: Absolutely. Medina del Campo offers several longer routes for endurance training. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of San Antolín loop from Medina del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a moderate 8.1-mile (13.0 km) trail, ideal for longer runs.

Q: Are there any specific natural features to look out for on the running trails?
A: Beyond the open fields and pine forests, keep an eye out for typical flora such as thyme, lupine, and broom along paths like the Camino de las Salinas. Birdwatchers might also spot species like magpies, woodpeckers, and thrushes in these natural areas.
tips: The Castillo de la Mota, also known as Castillo de La Mota, is a medieval fortress located in the town of Medina del Campo, in the province of Valladolid, Spain. Its name comes from its location on a high hill, a mote, from where it overlooks the city and its surroundings.

It is a historic building that stands as one of the most emblematic cultural treasures of the city.
The collegiate church is, along with the La Mota Castle, one of the most important historical buildings in Medina del Campo.
Although its main part shows characteristics of the late Gothic, its construction is complex and covers styles from the beginning of the 16th century to the 18th century.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the City tour through Medina del Campo?
faqs.answer: The estimated jogging duration for this 4.06-kilometer route is approximately 25 minutes. If you prefer a more leisurely pace, it's estimated to take about 1 hour and 4 minutes to walk.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as an 'easy' jogging tour, suitable for all fitness levels. It features minimal elevation changes and does not require good fitness or sure-footedness, making it ideal for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this city tour?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly urban and easily accessible, consisting mainly of streets, roads, and footpaths. You can expect a smooth and urban-friendly surface, perfect for jogging without significant technical challenges.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This city tour immerses you in the medieval charm of Medina del Campo. You will pass by significant historical landmarks such as the stunning Castle of La Mota, the Collegiate Church of San Antolín, Plaza Mayor, San Martin Street, the Royal Butcher Building, and the Church of San Miguel.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the City tour through Medina del Campo?
faqs.answer: As an urban route, parking is generally available in various public parking areas within Medina del Campo. You can typically find parking near the city center or major landmarks like the Castle of La Mota, which are convenient starting points for the tour.

faqs.question: Is the City tour through Medina del Campo dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, as a city tour primarily on public streets and footpaths, dogs are generally welcome, provided they are kept on a leash. Please be mindful of local regulations regarding pets in specific areas or buildings you might pass.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this jogging tour?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay any entrance fees to jog this city tour. It utilizes public paths and streets within Medina del Campo.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The route is accessible year-round due to its urban nature and minimal elevation changes. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summers can be warm, so consider early morning or late evening runs, while winters are generally mild but can be chilly.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of several official trails, including the Camino de Santiago del Sureste, Ruta de Carlos V, Etapa 25: Medina del Campo - Sieteiglesias de Trabancos, and Etapa 24: Arévalo - Medina del Campo.

faqs.question: Is the City tour through Medina del Campo a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this city tour is designed as a loop, allowing you to start and finish at the same point after exploring the historical sights of Medina del Campo.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: As an urban route through the city center, you will find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops along or very close to the trail, especially around the Plaza Mayor, offering plenty of opportunities for refreshments.
